Name: Experimental Rocket Propulsion Lab (ERPL)
Relevance: Focuses on the design and testing of high-power liquid and hybrid rocket engines like Project Harpy and Aquila.

Name: Rocket Development Lab (RDL)
Relevance: Develops solid-propellant sounding rockets for competitions like IREC and Spaceport America Cup.

Name: Team ECHO (Exploration for Ceres Habitation Operations)
Relevance: A student team designing human mission architectures for the dwarf planet Ceres for the NASA RASC-AL competition.

Name: EagleSat II
Relevance: A CubeSat mission developing satellite communication systems and conducting cosmic ray research in low Earth orbit.

Name: Space Physics Research Groups
Relevance: Conducts academic research in astrophysics, including gravitational waves and neutron star accretion physics.

Name: AIAA Design Build Fly (Coppertails)
Relevance: Designs and builds unmanned aerial vehicles for the international AIAA DBF competition cycles.

Name: VEX Robotics Club
Relevance: Participates in VEX U competitions involving the design and programming of autonomous robotic systems.

Name: Jim and Linda Lee Planetarium
Relevance: A facility for astronomical outreach and student-led research in interactive planetarium technology.

Name: High-Altitude Lab
Relevance: Simulates physiological challenges of flight and high-altitude environments for research and training.

Name: Eaglenautics
Relevance: Competes in the SAE Aero Design West competition, focusing on heavy-lift aeronautical engineering.

Name: UAS Lab
Relevance: Researches Uncrewed Aircraft Systems integrated with LiDAR and multispectral imaging for environmental monitoring.

Name: Atmospheric Physics / START Project
Relevance: Conducts airborne research and technology projects focused on atmospheric dynamics and turbulence.

Name: SEDS (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space)
Relevance: Promotes space exploration through advocacy and student involvement in space-related projects.

Name: AIAA Student Branch
Relevance: Professional organization branch providing networking and resources for aerospace engineering students.

Name: Optical Observatory
Relevance: Uses telescopes to conduct observational astronomy and astrophysical data collection.

Name: Team MEAGLE
Relevance: A former RASC-AL team that designed Martian environmental and life examination mission concepts.

Name: DRIP (Debris Recognition and Identification Platform)
Relevance: Researches machine learning methods for characterizing and identifying space debris.

Name: Prescott Astronomy Club
Relevance: Engages students in stargazing events and educational activities related to planetary science.

Name: Preflight Immersion Lab (PILOT)
Relevance: Uses VR technology for immersive flight training and aerospace procedural simulations.

Name: Golden Eagles Flight Team
Relevance: Highly competitive flight team focused on aeronautical performance and aviation excellence.
